howItWorks.intro: This photo file size calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: File Size
rules.explanation: Megapixels multiplied by compression factor per format.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How big is a RAW file?
faq.answer: Typically 1.5-2× the megapixel count in MB (e.g., 24MP = 36-48MB RAW).

faq.question: JPEG vs RAW?
faq.answer: RAW retains all sensor data for editing. JPEG is compressed and smaller.

faq.question: Is the Photo Storage Size Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard photography formulas trusted by photographers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What camera megapixels should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate camera megapixels value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Photo Storage Size Calculator?
sections.body: The Photo Storage Size Calculator is a free online tool designed for photographers who need quick, accurate calculations in the photography space. By entering your camera megapixels, file format, number of photos, you get instant results including size per photo, total storage, 32gb cards needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting size per photo right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In photography, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by photographers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to capture better images with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your camera megapixels and need to find the right size per photo. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Camera Megapixels
howToSteps.description: Type or select your camera megapixels in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your File Format
howToSteps.description: Type or select your file format in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Number of Photos
howToSteps.description: Type or select your number of photos in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your size per photo, total storage, 32gb cards needed. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your camera megapixels before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
